content:
- Read current task index and done index.
- Saw active tasks are all currently failing (`<passes>false</passes>`), mostly one high-priority docs story plus two managed-start-intent tasks.
- Next I am checking blockers and done-state so selection follows the mandatory precedence exactly.

- Checked active task files for blocker tags and priority.
- No active bug tasks are present in `.ralph/current_tasks.md`, `.ralph/current_tasks_done.md` is effectively empty, and no `<blocked_by>` tags are active for the listed candidates.
- Highest-priority selectable work is the high-priority docs story; the most logical next task is the first task in that story: establish the Diataxis/K2 workflow before downstream docs tasks.

- Started by reading Ralph state.
- .ralph/current_task.txt points to .ralph/tasks/story-build-docs-diataxis-from-zero/01-task-establish-diataxis-reread-and-draft-loop.md.
- Progress log read returned no prior visible content for codex.
- Next I am reading the task file and scanning minimal repo structure so I can write a concrete plan and stop at TO BE VERIFIED as instructed.

- Read the core docs skills: create-docs, ask-k2-docs, and update-docs.
- Read downstream story tasks 02 through 05 to see how this task should constrain later runs.
- They already encode much of the required loop, so the plan likely needs to tighten task 01 around consistency, verification, and maybe downstream wording updates rather than invent a brand new method.

- Read the full story task set and the create-docs / ask-k2-docs / update-docs skills.
- Confirmed docs/drafts/ is currently empty.
- Wrote a detailed implementation plan into .ralph/tasks/story-build-docs-diataxis-from-zero/01-task-establish-diataxis-reread-and-draft-loop.md.
- The plan ends with TO BE VERIFIED exactly as instructed.
- Stopped immediately after planning; did not execute the plan, run validations, or modify downstream task files yet.

- Read task 01 and found it already in the TO BE VERIFIED phase.
- Performed the skeptical review against downstream tasks 02 through 08 and the create-docs / ask-k2-docs / update-docs skills.
- Changed the implementation plan in task 01 before promoting it: added an explicit docs/drafts baseline check, limited downstream task edits to proven inconsistencies only, and added explicit checkbox-ticking/task-bookkeeping during execution.
- Replaced TO BE VERIFIED with NOW EXECUTE.
- Did not execute the plan yet, did not run validations, and did not edit downstream tasks in this turn.
- Next turn should read the task file, see NOW EXECUTE, and execute the plan directly without re-exploration unless the written plan itself requires it.

- Read prior progress and current task pointer.
- Confirmed the active task is story-build-docs-diataxis-from-zero/01-task-establish-diataxis-reread-and-draft-loop.md.
- Reading the task file now to honor its current phase marker exactly.

- Confirmed task 01 is in NOW EXECUTE.
- Reading downstream story tasks 02 through 08, docs/drafts baseline, and the create-docs / ask-k2-docs / update-docs skill instructions before editing.
- Next step is to patch task 01 plus any downstream tasks whose wording is weaker or inconsistent.

- Patched task 01 into a stricter workflow contract, added docs/drafts/diataxis-run-checklist.md, and tightened tasks 02 through 08 so prior prose cannot be treated as truth without repo re-checking.
- Re-reading the edited files skeptically now before running repo validations.

- make check and make test both passed.
- make test-long entered a long quiet phase; I checked the process table separately to confirm whether the wrapper is still live instead of assuming success.

- make test-long failed in gate test_long.docker_smoke_single due to timeout after 600s, not a normal test assertion.
- Investigating gate evidence and Docker state now to determine whether the smoke script hung due to environment or needs a repo fix.

- Probed the single-node smoke path directly: it passed in about 20 seconds.
- The original test-long timeout appears transient or resource-related rather than a persistent logic failure.
- Cleaning stale smoke-test Docker containers and volumes before rerunning make test-long.
